instruction: Go up the stairs and take a left. Walk towards the couch and chairs in the sitting area. Walk through the sitting area and stop in front of the first door to the balcony on your left.

system_prompt:
You are controlling a robot in a real indoor environment (a photorealistic 3D scan of a building). You interact only through these tools:

- observe(): look through the robot's forward-facing camera (RGB image plus a clearance readout: meters to the nearest obstacle in the left/center/right thirds of the view; 10.0 = open).
- step(actions): execute movement actions in order. 0 = STOP (permanently ends the episode — declares you have reached the goal), 1 = move forward 0.25 m, 2 = turn left 15 degrees, 3 = turn right 15 degrees.
- look_around(): one call returning four labeled views (ahead / right / behind / left); rotates 360 degrees and restores your heading (costs 24 turn steps).

Your task is to follow this navigation instruction to its endpoint:

"Go up the stairs and take a left. Walk towards the couch and chairs in the sitting area. Walk through the sitting area and stop in front of the first door to the balcony on your left. "

Rules:
- Alternate observing and stepping: look, decide where the instruction wants you to go next, move, look again.
- You have a budget of 500 movement actions; each step() result reports roughly how many remain.
- You succeed only if you issue action 0 (STOP) while within 3 meters of the instruction's endpoint. STOP is permanent — issue it only when you believe you are at the goal.
- Turning in place (e.g. step([2,2,2,2,2,2])) is a cheap way to look around when unsure.
- Work autonomously until you stop; nobody can answer questions.
first_prompt: Begin navigating. Call observe() first to see where you are.

tool_schemas.name: observe
tool_schemas.description:
Look through the robot's forward-facing camera.

Returns the current egocentric RGB view plus a clearance readout: distance in meters to the nearest obstacle in the left/center/right thirds of the view (10.0 = open, 10 m or more). If the goal object is centered ahead, clearance "center" is your true distance to it. Pure read — does not advance the simulator or consume step budget.

tool_schemas.name: look_around
tool_schemas.description:
Scan the surroundings in ONE call: rotates a full 360 degrees and
    returns four labeled views — ahead (0°), right (+90°), behind (+180°),
    left (+270°) — then restores the original heading exactly.

    Costs 24 low-level turn steps from the step budget but only one tool
    call. Use this at junctions and decision points instead of turning 15
    degrees at a time; your position and final heading are unchanged.

tool_schemas.name: step
tool_schemas.description:
Execute a sequence of movement actions, in order.

Actions: 0 = STOP (permanently ENDS the episode — issue it only when you believe the robot is within 3 meters of the goal), 1 = move forward 0.25 m, 2 = turn left 15 degrees, 3 = turn right 15 degrees.

Executes sequentially and halts early if the episode ends. Returns how many actions ran, total steps taken, remaining budget, and whether the episode is over. The camera view changes after stepping — call observe() to see the result. Note: when plenty of budget remains, your FIRST STOP request is withheld pending a placement check — call step([0]) again to confirm and execute it.

text: Begin navigating. Call observe() first to see where you are.
I'll start by observing my surroundings.
This looks like a rustic wooden structure. I need to find stairs to go up. Let me scan around to understand my surroundings.
The right view (+90°) shows stairs going up. Let me turn right to face the stairs.
I can see stairs going up on the left side. Let me turn slightly left to align with the stairs and then go up.
Now I can see the stairs directly ahead of me going up. Let me climb them.
I've climbed the stairs and reached the top. I can see an open landing area ahead. Let me continue forward to reach the top of the stairs.
I'm now on the upper level. The instruction says to take a left after going up the stairs. Let me look around to understand the layout up here.

Looking at the views, the "ahead" and "left" views show open areas that could lead to a sitting area. The instruction says take a left after the stairs. The left view (+270°) shows an open room with what could be furniture. Let me turn left to head toward the sitting area.

Center clearance is only 0.6m (a post/beam in front). There's open space to the right. This area is quite cluttered. Let me look at the "ahead" direction (which was more open) again. The original ahead had a nicer open floor. Let me reconsider - the sitting area with couches/chairs. Let me turn to find the more open path. Let me check right a bit.

This area is very cluttered with what looks like debris/artifacts. This doesn't look like a clean sitting area with couches. Let me reconsider. The "ahead" (original heading) view showed a cleaner open floor area with lit windows. Perhaps the sitting area is in the direction I was originally heading. Let me go back to face the original ahead direction and explore the more open area toward the windows.
I'm now back at the original heading (ahead). This showed an open floor with windows. Let me move forward into this open area to look for the sitting area.

Now I can see a much clearer view. There are large windows/glass doors on the right (possibly balcony doors), and there appears to be furniture in the left/center area (chairs, tables). The open floor space suggests this is a living area. Let me move forward toward the furniture area.

I can see furniture on the left (there's a white chair/couch), and glass doors/windows on the right side which could be the balcony doors. The instruction says to walk through the sitting area and stop in front of the first door to the balcony on the left. Let me look around to understand the sitting area and balcony doors better.

Now I have a good picture. The right view (+90°) shows glass paned doors — these are likely balcony doors. The left view (+270°) shows the sitting area with couches and chairs. 

The instruction says: walk through the sitting area, stop in front of the first door to the balcony on your LEFT. So I need to continue walking through the sitting area (which is to my left/ahead-left), keeping the balcony doors on my left. 

Let me turn toward the sitting area (left) and move through it, with the balcony doors coming up on the left. Actually, let me first head into the sitting area. Turn left.
Now I'm facing the sitting area. I can see chairs and furniture ahead, and there's a large window/glass area in the center-back which could be the balcony area. Let me walk into the sitting area.

I'm now in the sitting area. I can see chairs/furniture around and large windows/glass doors ahead which lead to a balcony. There's a post directly in front of me (center clearance 1.0). Right side is more open (3.6m). Let me steer right a bit to avoid the post and continue toward the sitting area with couches. Let me turn right slightly.

Now I have a clearer path ahead (center 4.2m). I can see the sitting area with couches/chairs and there are large glass doors/windows ahead which lead to the balcony. There appear to be balcony doors on the left area too. Let me continue forward through the sitting area toward the balcony doors.

Now I clearly see the sitting area with couches and chairs, and there are large glass doors/windows ahead which are the balcony doors. This is exactly the sitting area described. I can see multiple glass doors leading to the balcony. 

The instruction says stop in front of the first door to the balcony on my left. I need to walk through the sitting area. Let me look around to identify the balcony doors more precisely.

Now I can see clearly. The "ahead" view shows the sitting area with couches/chairs and a wall of glass doors that lead to the balcony. The instruction says to walk through the sitting area and stop in front of the first door to the balcony on my left.

The glass doors are ahead of me. As I walk through the sitting area, the balcony doors would be on my left if I orient properly. Let me move forward toward the sitting area/couches and the glass doors.

I'm now right in the sitting area with couches and chairs. Ahead of me is a wall of glass doors that lead to the balcony. The couches are directly ahead/right. 

The instruction says to walk through the sitting area and stop in front of the first door to the balcony on my left. The glass doors are the balcony doors. I need to identify them and position myself so the first door is on my left. 

Let me move a bit forward and to the left to walk through the sitting area, keeping the balcony doors on my left side. Let me first turn slightly left to walk along the room parallel to the doors.

Now I can see the balcony doors clearly - they are the glass paned French doors ahead of me and to the right. There's a couch/chair area in the center. The instruction says walk through the sitting area and stop in front of the first door to the balcony on my left.

I need to reorient. The balcony doors are ahead of me. If I walk along the room, the doors should be on my left. Let me turn so I'm walking parallel with the doors on my left. Currently the doors are ahead-right. Let me turn right to face along the wall of doors, then the doors will be on my left as I proceed... 

Actually, let me reconsider the layout. Let me walk toward the doors first to get closer, then determine which is the "first" door. Let me move toward them.

Now I can see the full wall of balcony French doors ahead. There appear to be multiple doors. The sitting area (couches, chairs, ottoman) is in front of the doors. 

The instruction: "Walk through the sitting area and stop in front of the first door to the balcony on your left." So I should walk through the sitting area, and the balcony doors will be arranged along a wall on my left. Let me position myself so I'm walking along the room with doors to my left. 

The doors span the wall ahead. If I turn to walk parallel (turn right so I face down the room), the doors will be on my left. Let me turn right to walk along the door wall.

Now I'm facing down the length of the room. The balcony doors (glass French doors) are on the left wall. The sitting area with couches and chairs continues ahead. This matches walking through the sitting area with the balcony doors on my left.

The "first door to the balcony on my left" would be the first of the French doors along this left wall. Let me walk forward through the sitting area and identify the first door on my left. Let me move forward a bit.

Now I can clearly see the wall of balcony doors on my left. These are large glass French doors leading to the balcony. On the left there are multiple French doors. The "first door to the balcony on my left" - since I've entered the sitting area and I'm now walking through it, the first door I encounter on my left is the one at the beginning of the wall.

Let me turn to face the doors on my left to confirm I'm in front of the first one.
Hmm, I've turned too far to the left. I now see a wall/staircase area and only the right portion shows the glass doors. Let me turn back right a bit to face the balcony doors directly.

Now I'm facing the wall of balcony doors. I can see the glass French doors that lead to the balcony. There's a couch/chair in front. This is the sitting area with the balcony doors.

Now I need to identify the "first door to the balcony on my left." Let me reconsider my orientation. When I entered this area, I was walking through the sitting area. The doors span this wall. Let me determine which is the "first" door as I walk through.

Let me get closer to the doors to see them clearly and identify individual doors. Let me move forward toward the doors.

I can see the wall of balcony French doors clearly ahead. There's a large glass door structure - it looks like a set of French doors in the center leading to the balcony, flanked by windows. There are couches/ottomans in front of me in the sitting area.

The center French doors appear to be the main balcony access. The instruction says "the first door to the balcony on your left." Given my path walking through the sitting area, I approached with the door wall on my left. 

I'm now essentially in front of the balcony doors. The center clearance is 4.0m to the doors. Let me approach the doors more, aiming for the leftmost/first door. Let me steer slightly left toward the first door and move forward, navigating around the furniture.

Now I can see the balcony French doors clearly. There's a set of double French doors in the center-right, and windows to the left. The doors lead out to the balcony (I can see greenery/light outside). 

I'm now quite close to the doors. The center door set is the main balcony door. As I walk through the sitting area, the "first door on my left" — I need to identify. There seem to be doors along this wall. The set ahead-right appears to be a door pair.

Let me look at the full wall by scanning to see how many doors there are and identify the first one.

Now I can see the wall of balcony doors extending to the right. There's a French door on the right (the glass paned door) leading to the balcony. To the left is the sitting area with more furniture and the staircase.

I think the layout is: I've walked through the sitting area, and the balcony doors are along the right/ahead wall. But the instruction says the first door should be on my LEFT.

Let me reconsider. When walking through the sitting area toward the far end, the balcony doors would be on one side. Let me reorient to walk down the room with doors on my left. Let me turn right to face down the room so the doors are on my left.

Now I'm facing down the length of the room. The balcony doors (glass French doors) are along the left wall, and the sitting area with couches/chairs extends ahead and to the right. This is the correct orientation: walking through the sitting area with balcony doors on my left.

The "first door to the balcony on my left" would be the first French door I reach as I walk forward. Looking at the left wall, I can see the French doors. The one nearest to me on the left is the "first door." 

Let me walk forward a little through the sitting area to be aligned in front of the first door.

Now I can clearly see the balcony French doors on my left. There's a set of French doors (double glass paned doors) directly to my left that lead out to the balcony (I can see greenery outside). This appears to be the first door to the balcony on my left as I walk through the sitting area.

Let me turn left to face this door and confirm I'm in front of it.

Now I'm facing the balcony door on the right side of my view. The French doors with glass panes lead out to the balcony (greenery visible outside). Let me turn a bit more to center on the door in front of me.

Now I'm facing the balcony French doors directly. I can see the glass paned double doors that lead to the balcony. The center clearance is 2.9m to the doors. 

This is the first door to the balcony on my left (as I was walking through the sitting area). I'm about 2.9m away. Let me move a bit closer to be clearly "in front of" the door, then stop. I need to be within 3m of the endpoint - I already am, but let me get right in front of the door.

I'm now right in front of the balcony French doors. I can clearly see the large glass paned doors that lead out to the balcony (greenery visible outside). I'm about 2.4m from the door, which is within the 3m goal range.

I have walked up the stairs, taken a left, walked through the sitting area with couches and chairs, and I'm now standing in front of the first balcony door on my left. This matches the instruction's endpoint. Let me stop here.

texts: {"stop_withheld": true, "message": "STOP not executed (first request). You still have 39 tool calls \u2014 verify placement before committing: (1) look_around(); (2) re-read the instruction's FINAL clause word by word; (3) apply the placement rules (several similar spots -> the one farther along your travel direction; 'all the way / to the end' -> keep going until center clearance < 1.5 m; 'door/doorway' -> stand at the opening). Move if a better spot exists, then call step([0]) again to execute it.", "tool_calls_used": 61, "tool_calls_remaining": 39}

text: The STOP was withheld for verification. Let me re-read the final clause: "stop in front of the first door to the balcony on your left." I'm in front of a balcony door. Let me verify with a look_around to confirm this is the first door and I'm well-positioned.

Let me analyze the views:
- Ahead (0°): The balcony French doors with glass panes - this is a balcony door.
- Right (+90°): Shows more of the room with a fireplace and sitting area, and windows/doors continuing.
- Behind (+180°): The sitting area I came through with couches and chairs.
- Left (+270°): The staircase and dining/other area.

I'm facing the balcony doors directly ahead. The behind view confirms I walked through the sitting area (couches/chairs) to reach here. The balcony doors span the wall. 

This is the first door to the balcony reached after walking through the sitting area. I'm about 2.4m from the door, which is within 3m. The placement looks correct - I'm in front of the balcony door. Let me confirm the STOP.
